commit.message:
Patch 068: Cap remaining OOM-inducing loops in fromxml/mluc paths

Cap 4 uncapped allocation loops causing OOM in icc_fromxml_fuzzer:
- ProfileSeqDesc XML parsing: 256 entries max
- ResponseCurveSet XML parsing: 64 curves, 8192 measurements max
- MultiProcessElement XML parsing: 256 elements max
- CIccTagMultiLocalizedUnicode::Read binary path: 4096 nNumRec max

Root cause: CIccLocalizedUnicode copy ctor at IccTagBasic.cpp:7123
allocating 659K copies (1.37GB, 44% of heap) from uncapped loops.

Extends patch 067 (which capped mluc XML path, ProfileSeqId, Dict).

CWE-789: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: plug resource leaks in SNMP session setup and poll paths (#418)

Fixes #415

Four resource leaks in snmp.c and poller.c:

1. snmp.c: `session.peername` not `strdup()`'d, dangling reference after
`snmp_sess_open()`
2. snmp.c: `securityAuthProto`/`securityPrivProto` OIDs leaked on 4
error paths and happy path
3. snmp.c: PDU leaked in `snmp_get_base()` and `snmp_getnext()` when
`snmp_parse_oid()` fails
4. poller.c: `sprintf` overflow in `exec_poll()`, switched to `snprintf`

All declarations at block top per project convention. Tested with gcc
and clang.

---------

Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: TheWitness <thewitness@cacti.net>

commit.message:
Fix TypeError in `_copy_output` when closure captures `FunctionWrapper` with `friendly_tangents=true` (#1024)

* Initial plan

* Fix TypeError in _copy_output when closure captures FunctionWrapper with friendly_tangents=true

Added special dispatch methods in `_copy_output` and `_copy_to_output!!` for `Type`,
`Core.TypeName`, and `Module` values so they are returned as-is (reference-semantic).

Previously, these Julia runtime internals would be deep-copied via `jl_new_struct_uninit`
/ `jl_new_structv`, which fails for `Core.TypeName` (has field layout constraints that
cause a TypeError when setting type-valued fields).

Added regression tests: direct unit tests for the new methods, a mutable-struct-with-Type-field
copy test, and an end-to-end test with `prepare_pullback_cache` + `friendly_tangents=true`
on a closure capturing a struct with a `Type` field.

fix(hot-reload): atomic resolver registration, volatile dynamic RunTest

P1 fixes (expert panel round 3):
- resolverRegistered: ref bool → Interlocked.CompareExchange(ref int).
  Assembly resolve callbacks fire on arbitrary CLR threads — plain ref
  read/write has a TOCTOU race that could double-register the handler.
  Pattern already used in DevReloadInjector.fs for consistency.
- latestDynamicRunTest: mutable → ref + Volatile.Read/Interlocked.Exchange.
  Writer (file watcher ThreadPool) and reader (HTTP handler thread) need
  memory barrier for ARM64 .NET correctness. x86 TSO model masks the bug.

commit.message:
feat: synthesize constructor with field assignments for primary constructors

When a C# class or struct uses a primary constructor, the converter now
always synthesizes an explicit constructor that assigns parameters to
their corresponding backing fields. Previously a constructor was only
generated when a base class call was present, leaving classes without
base types unable to set their synthesized fields.

Changes:
- ConvertClass: always emit a constructor for primary ctor params with
  this.field = param assignments; keep base call initializer when present
- ConvertStruct: same pattern (no base call possible for structs)
- Remove default values from synthesized fields (defaults stay on ctor params)
- Add PrimaryConstructorTests covering class, struct, record, base class,
  explicit member, and explicit ctor scenarios

fix(hot-reload): P0 assembly over-loading, thread safety, module scoring; P1 EndsWith, TOCTOU

P0 fixes (expert panel identified):
- resolveAssembly: use AssemblyName.GetAssemblyName() to inspect version
  metadata BEFORE loading. Previously Assembly.LoadFrom on ALL candidates
  polluted the AppDomain. Now only the best candidate is loaded.
- assemblySearchPaths: ResizeArray → ConcurrentDictionary for thread safety.
  Assembly resolve callbacks fire on arbitrary CLR threads.
- Module scoring: Seq.tryHead → Seq.fold max 0. tryHead took the score
  from the first open module; fold finds the best match across all modules.

P1 fixes:
- Pre-filter .Contains → .EndsWith(Ordinal) for consistency with scoring.
  .Contains matched 'getUser' inside 'getUsers'; .EndsWith does not.
- perFileCts: manual TryGetValue+Cancel+Dispose → ConcurrentDictionary.AddOrUpdate
  to eliminate TOCTOU race on rapid saves.

8 new tests (65 total): thread safety, module scoring max, EndsWith pre-filter.
